title: Flash Jack From Gundagai
content:
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve shore at Burrabogie, and Iï؟½ve shore at Toganmain,
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve shore at big Willandra and upon the old Coleraine,
آ آ آ آ But before the shearinï؟½ was over Iï؟½ve wished myself back, again
آ آ آ آ Shearinï؟½ for old Tom Patterson, on the One Tree Plain.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ All among the wool, boys,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Keep your wide blades full, boys,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ I can do a respectable tally myself whenever I like to try,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ But they know me round the back blocks as Flash Jack from Gundagai.

آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve shore at big Willandra and Iï؟½ve shore at Tilberoo,
آ آ آ آ And once I drew my blades, my boys, upon the famed Barcoo,
آ آ آ آ At Cowan Downs and Trida, as far as Moulamein,
آ آ آ آ But I always was glad to get back again to the One Tree Plain.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Chorus: All among the wool, &c.

آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve pinked ï؟½em with the Wolseleys and Iï؟½ve rushed with B-bows, too,
آ آ آ آ And shaved ï؟½em in the grease, my boys, with the grass seed showing through.
آ آ آ آ But I never slummed my pen, my lads, whateï؟½er it might contain,
آ آ آ آ While shearinï؟½ for old Tom Patterson, on the One Tree Plain.

آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve been whalinï؟½ up the Lachlan, and Iï؟½ve dossed on Cooperï؟½s Creek,
آ آ آ آ And once I rung Cudjingie shed, and blued it in a week.
آ آ آ آ But when Gabriel blows his trumpet, lads, Iï؟½ll catch the morning train,
آ آ آ آ And Iï؟½ll push for old Tom Pattersonï؟½s, on the One Tree Plain.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
